label: Badge
purpose: Maps a semantic variant prop to existing badge/tag classes in homepage.css. Renders labels, category tags, and status indicators. Introduces no new CSS. Has no interactive states — never use as a button or link.

props.name: label
props.description: Visible text content. Plain string only — no HTML or JSX. Badge has no slot.

props.name: variant
props.description: Controls which CSS class and DOM structure is rendered. coming-soon has a different DOM (two spans) — see allowedHTMLOutput.

props.name: ...rest
props.description: Spread onto the root span element. Acceptable uses: style overrides, data-* attributes. Do not pass class — use the variant system instead.

variants.description: Yellow pill, black text, border, full radius. Category label for play-cards. Use on --bg-primary or --bg-surface only.
Yellow background, black text, border, xs radius. Category label for diary cards. Use on --bg-primary only.
Dark background (--color-gray-900), --fg-on-dark-label text, xs radius. Row type label in the work section. Use on --bg-dark only.
Black pill (--color-black bg), yellow text (--fg-on-dark-accent), full radius, border. Skills/tag pills in the about section. Use on the dark about card surface only.
Red text (--color-error) with an animated dot. DOM is structurally different — see allowedHTMLOutput. Indicates an item is not yet live.

renderRule: Always renders as <span>. tag, diary-tag, work-tag, skill: single <span class='[variant-class]'>. coming-soon: outer <span class='work-coming-soon-badge'> containing an inner <span class='work-coming-soon-dot'> followed by the label text.
accessibility: Badge has no interactive states — do not give it onClick, tabIndex, or role='button'
tag and diary-tag use yellow background on light surfaces — the black text (#0A0A0A) provides sufficient contrast
skill uses yellow text (--fg-on-dark-accent) on a black background — safe per WCAG AA
coming-soon uses --color-error (red) as its only contrast signal — do not remove the dot animation as it is also a motion cue
label must be plain text — screen readers read it directly
whenToUse: tag: category labels on play-cards in the Playground section
diary-tag: entry type labels on diary cards
work-tag: row type labels in the work section (on dark surface)
skill: skills/tag list in the about section
coming-soon: any item that is not yet live — indicates status, not category
whenNotToUse: tag or diary-tag on dark surfaces — yellow badge on dark background lacks contrast and is contextually wrong
skill on light surfaces — black pill with yellow text on white has poor visual hierarchy and is out of context
work-tag on light surfaces — dark pill on white is technically readable but contextually wrong
coming-soon where the parent already sets color: --color-error — avoids double-red
As a navigation element, button, or link — Badge is display-only with no interactive states
When content needs to change dynamically or carry event handlers — use a raw element instead

knownRawCSSMimics.migrationNote: AboutSection.astro is an Astro file — can import Badge and use <Badge variant='skill' label={s} /> in the map.
WorkSection.jsx is a React island — cannot import Badge.astro. Must use raw CSS class.
WorkSection.jsx is a React island — cannot import Badge.astro. Also: raw usage in JSX manually includes work-coming-soon-dot as a separate <span>, matching the Badge DOM output.

usageConstraints.reason: Badge.astro cannot be imported into .jsx React islands (WorkSection.jsx, Footer.jsx, Header.jsx, TweaksPanel.jsx). In JSX, use the raw CSS class on a <span> and manually replicate the DOM for coming-soon (outer span + inner dot span).
